composition.Programmer 1:
Implement backend code in app.py
- Develop the main backend Python application file.
- The website should be accessible via local port 5000, with the first page accessible at route '/' (Dashboard).
- Implement routing for all eight frontend pages, ensuring each route corresponds to the frontend code.
- Implement file read/write operations for all data files in the 'data/' directory, starting from the first line, using plain text without encryption.
- Support all functionalities  current weather, weekly forecasts, location search, weather alerts, air quality, saved locations, and settings.
- Frontend code files to be supported  dashboard.html, current_weather.html, weekly_forecast.html, location_search.html, weather_alerts.html, air_quality.html, saved_locations.html, settings.html.
- Incorporate the original task’s supplementary condition  no authentication required; all features directly accessible.
composition.Programmer 2:
Develop frontend templates in the 'templates/' directory
- Complete all eight HTML template files  dashboard.html, current_weather.html, weekly_forecast.html, location_search.html, weather_alerts.html, air_quality.html, saved_locations.html, settings.html.
- Ensure all URLs in the frontend code are routes defined in the backend, not direct HTML file names.
- Implement the page designs and elements exactly as specified in the requirements document, including all IDs and page titles.
- Ensure the Dashboard page is the starting page of the website.
composition.Programmer 3:
Develop static assets in the 'static/' directory
- Implement CSS styles in css/styles.css for consistent styling across all pages.
- Implement JavaScript in js/scripts.js for client-side interactivity and navigation.
- Ensure that client-side scripts support navigation via backend routes and enhance user experience without breaking the routing logic.
